pages.title: Installation | Playwright
contentBlocks: Playwright Test was created specifically to accommodate the needs of end-to-end testing.
Install Playwright using npm, yarn, or pnpm to get started quickly.
The install command downloads Playwright binaries for Chromium, Firefox and WebKit.
System requirements: Node.js 18+, Windows 10+, macOS 13 Ventura, Debian 11, Ubuntu 22.04.

pages.title: Locators | Playwright
contentBlocks: Locators are the central piece of Playwright's auto-waiting and retry-ability.
Use page.getByRole() to locate by ARIA role, page.getByText() to locate by text content.
Locators are strict by default. If a query selector matches more than one element, Playwright will throw an error.
Auto-waiting means Playwright automatically waits for elements to be visible, enabled, and stable before interacting.
Use the locator.filter() method to narrow down results based on additional conditions.

callouts.content: Locators are strict. If more than one element matches a locator, Playwright will throw an error. Use filter() to narrow down results.

pages.title: Assertions | Playwright
contentBlocks: Playwright uses the expect library for test assertions.
Auto-retrying assertions will keep checking the condition until it passes or the timeout is reached.
Use expect(locator).toBeVisible() to assert that an element is visible on the page.
Soft assertions do not immediately terminate the test — the test will continue after a soft assertion failure.
Non-retrying assertions like expect(value).toBe() execute immediately without retrying.
